description: The Philadelphia Greek Mob was a loose-knit Greek-American criminal faction operating in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, closely allied with the Italian-American Philadelphia crime family. Active primarily from the 1970s to the 1980s, it engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, extortion, loansharking, and illegal gambling. The group was led by Chelsais "Steve" Bouras until his assassination in 1981, after which its influence declined.
